user_system_path: Issue #364 — make the workday-presence 15-min heartbeat actually fire in production. PR #365 (f173bdc) removed the dead CRON_SECRET-over-HTTP path. GitHub Actions is capped on this private repo (runners never start), so scheduling moves to a free external cron service hitting the existing Vercel route; route + CRON_SECRET already exist, no new spend. This PR removes the dead Actions schedule and ships the setup runbook; owner creates the external cron job to close out.
allowed_files_raw: the trigger-runner workflow (remove dead Actions schedule, document external-cron heartbeat) plus control-plane files
forbidden_files_raw: dashboard UI, components — this is backend cron-scheduling plumbing only; no product/runtime logic change
required_browser_proof: not applicable — backend cron plumbing, no UI surface
acceptance_condition: PR merged; a free external cron job hits GET https://www.foldera.ai/api/cron/workday-presence-trigger-runner every 15 min with the Vercel CRON_SECRET and returns HTTP 200 with a quiet/dedup_suppressed/intervention outcome (no silent 401, no dependence on capped GitHub Actions).
stop_condition: Stop after the PR is merged and the owner has created the external cron job; issue #364 closes on the first confirmed HTTP 200 from the route.
anti_regression_checks: workday-presence-trigger-runner.yml no longer carries a schedule trigger that can never fire
the /api/cron/workday-presence-trigger-runner route and its CRON_SECRET auth are unchanged
next_command: Owner creates the external cron job (cron-job.org or equivalent) per the runbook; confirm first HTTP 200, then close issue #364 and return control plane to between-rungs.
